name: Box MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Box MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Box's content management platform — reading, uploading, searching, and organizing files and folders; managing permissions; and accessing Box's AI features.
use_cases: Reading and analyzing documents stored in Box from knowledge agents
Searching Box content for relevant files using Box AI
Uploading agent-generated documents and reports to Box
Managing file permissions and sharing for collaboration agents
Using Box AI to extract insights from stored documents
not_for: Teams using Google Drive, Dropbox, or SharePoint for storage
Simple file hosting without enterprise governance needs
best_when: An agent needs to read, manage, or search enterprise documents stored in Box — particularly when using Box AI for document intelligence.
avoid_when: Your organization uses Google Drive or SharePoint — use those integrations instead.

auth.notes: Server-side authentication via JWT (app users) or Client Credentials Grant for agent use. OAuth 2.0 for user-delegated. Scopes control resource access.

pricing.notes: Enterprise features require paid plans. Box AI requires Enterprise Plus or above. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: 1,000 API calls/minute per user token; 10 req/s for OAuth

known_agent_gotchas: JWT auth setup requires RSA key pair and Box app configuration — complex onboarding
App users vs actual users distinction — agents should use app users for automated workflows
File IDs are mandatory for all operations — must discover IDs before operating
Box AI requires Enterprise Plus license — verify plan before building AI features
Folder structure traversal requires recursive API calls
Webhook signatures for security — implement verification for inbound webhooks
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. JWT auth eliminates user passwords for agents. Fine-grained scopes. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI DSS — enterprise-grade content security.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. JWT app authentication with scopes. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI DSS. Enterprise content management security.
